text.title: rag-implementation Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of rag-implementation enforces.

rule.statement: System prompt MUST contain 'answer ONLY from the provided context' AND a fallback 'If the context does not contain the answer, say so.'
rule.rationale: Without these the model leaks training knowledge.

rule.statement: Implementation exposes all five chunker strategies (fixed / sentence / paragraph / semantic / md-header) selectable by config — do not hardcode one.
rule.rationale: Different doc types need different chunkers; switching cost must be one config line.

rule.statement: Query enhancement (expansion / HyDE / rewrite) is opt-in via config flag; default is plain query.
rule.rationale: Always-on enhancement adds latency and can degrade simple queries.

rule.statement: Generator returns citations in [Source: filename, page] format; pipeline parses + validates against retrieved chunk metadata.
Implementation tracks per-doc content hashes; re-embed only changed docs.
rule.rationale: Full re-embed on every update is too expensive at scale.

rule.statement: Skip when the team is on a non-Chroma DB and wants the specific store's idiom; use vector-database-setup + rag instead.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
